chief_complaint: A 50-year-old male presented with class III angina on exertion.
history: The patient reported a 2-year history of progressive exertional chest pain, relieved by rest. He denied any history of hypertension, diabetes, or hyperlipidemia. He was on aspirin and simvastatin.
exam: Vital signs: BP 140/90 mmHg, HR 80 bpm, RR 16/min. Physical examination revealed a normal cardiac examination.
labs: ECG: normal. CCTA: demonstrated a type IV dual left anterior descending artery and a split right coronary artery.
correct_diagnosis: Unique coexistence of dual left anterior descending artery and split right coronary artery
differentials: Unique coexistence of dual left anterior descending artery and split right coronary artery
correct_ranking: Unique coexistence of dual left anterior descending artery and split right coronary artery
